functions.name: automate.create_custom_email_body
functions.description: Creates a professional and personalized email body based on user requirements and dynamic inputs from previous steps or additional context.

args.user_input: Base description of the email content (string).
args.dynamic_inputs: Optional additional dynamic data (dict). These will be directly included in the email prompt.

returns.email_body: The generated email body as a string, ready to use in communication steps.

functions.notes: Uses AI to create an email body. All dynamic inputs are interpolated directly into the email content. Includes user's business information if relevant.

functions.name: automate.generate_file_from_ai
functions.description: Generates a file (txt, md, css, html, docx, pptx) with content based on user input. Filename and file type are dynamically suggested by AI.

args.user_input: The description or instructions for the file content (string). Can specify style, format, structure, or type of file.

returns.file_path: The path to the generated file on disk (string).

functions.notes: Uses the AI model to generate both content and a suitable filename with extension. Supports structured content for Word (.docx) and PowerPoint (.pptx) files.

functions.name: automate.generate_email_reply
functions.description: Generates a polite, professional, and context-aware email reply based on a given previous email message.

args.previous_msg: The content of the previous email message (string).

returns.email_reply: The AI-generated email reply as a string.

functions.notes: Uses AI to produce a formal email response.

functions.name: automate.generate_chat_reply
functions.description: Generates a friendly and conversational reply based on a previous chat message. Accepts any number of dynamic keyword arguments (**args) such as intent, workflow, tone, or chat history for enhanced contextual responses.

args.previous_msg: The content of the previous chat message (string).
args.**args: Additional optional keyword arguments (dict). These can include dynamic_inputs,step data, workflow data, chat context, intent, or other runtime variables that help generate the best chat message.

returns.chat_reply: The AI-generated chat reply as a string.

functions.notes: Uses AI to generate a natural and contextually appropriate chat message. Automatically incorporates all **args into the AI prompt for richer contextual understanding.

functions.name: automate.search_knowledge_base
functions.description: Searches internal or user-uploaded documents using semantic vector search and returns the most relevant answer based on the query.

args.user_input: The user's query or question to search for in the knowledge base (string).

returns.response: The most relevant answer retrieved from the document sources, or null if no relevant result is found.

functions.notes: Use this ONLY when the user explicitly asks to search, retrieve, or answer from uploaded documents, files, or knowledge base. Do NOT use for general questions, reasoning, or when the answer can be generated without document retrieval. Uses vector similarity search (e.g., LanceDB) to retrieve context-aware answers from stored documents. Designed for RAG-based workflows.

functions.name: automate.generate_ai_content
functions.description: Generates creative or informational content such as stories, explanations, study material, preparation guides, summaries, ideas, articles, or conceptual learning content based on user input. This function is intended for narrative or informational content generation and NOT for generating structured questions, quizzes, MCQs, or mock exams.

args.**args: Optional keyword arguments such as tone, length, style, workflow context, dynamic_inputs, or other hints that guide the generation style and content.

returns.generated_content: AI-generated narrative or informational text based on the user request (string).

functions.notes: This function must NOT generate structured questions, quizzes, MCQs, or mock exams. If the user explicitly asks for questions, quizzes, tests, or mock exams, the system must instead use the 'automate.generate_questions' function.

functions.name: automate.generate_questions
functions.description: Generates structured questions for workflows. By default, creates normal (open-ended) questions. MCQ or Quiz questions are generated only when explicitly requested by the user.

args.user_input: User's request describing the questions or quiz to generate. The requested format (normal, MCQ, or quiz) MUST be explicitly stated here if anything other than normal questions is required.
args.**args: Optional keyword arguments used only as constraints such as count, tone, domain, difficulty, topic, or workflow context. These MUST NOT change the question format unless explicitly requested in user_input.

returns.questions: A JSON array. By default, each item is a normal question object. If a quiz is explicitly requested, the array may contain a single quiz object grouping multiple questions.

rules: Normal (open-ended) questions are the DEFAULT output.
MCQ questions are generated ONLY if explicitly requested using terms such as 'MCQ', 'multiple choice', or 'choose one'.
Quiz generation is triggered ONLY if explicitly requested using terms such as 'quiz', 'test', or 'assessment'.
Each normal or MCQ question MUST contain 'question' and 'answer': null.
MCQ questions MUST additionally contain an 'options' object with keys A, B, C, and D.
If a quiz is generated, it MUST be returned as a grouped structure inside the questions array.
No answers, explanations, scoring, or metadata are generated unless explicitly requested.
Output MUST be execution-ready and suitable for workflow updates and later answer injection.
notes: The **args field is constraint-only and never controls output format.
If multiple formats are mentioned, quiz takes precedence over MCQ.
If the request is ambiguous or unclear, default to normal questions.
This function is safe for automated workflow execution systems.

functions.name: automate.evaluate_answers
functions.description: Evaluates answers provided for generated questions and returns structured scoring, correctness labels, and analysis. Supports both open-ended (text) questions and MCQ questions. Optionally uses a provided answer key as the primary reference during evaluation.

args.questions: A JSON array of question objects containing at minimum: id, question, type (text|mcq), and user_answer. MCQ questions may include an options object.
args.answer_key_json: Optional JSON array containing reference answers or correct answers mapped by question_id. If provided, this is used as the primary reference during evaluation.

returns.summary: Aggregated evaluation metrics including total_questions, attempted, correct, incorrect, unanswered, total_score, max_score, percentage, and grade.
returns.results: A JSON array containing evaluation results for each question including score, correctness label, analysis, and a model_answer reference.

notes: The answer_key_json argument is optional and only influences evaluation if provided.
Reference answers should be treated as conceptual guidance rather than requiring exact wording matches.
Evaluation focuses on conceptual completeness rather than phrasing similarity.
The returned results array is designed for direct use in workflow engines, grading dashboards, or automated feedback systems.

functions.name: automate.review_content
functions.description: Reviews user-provided content against workflow AI instructions and context. Provides a professional evaluation indicating whether the content is acceptable, needs improvement, or is not acceptable, along with clear feedback and improvement guidance. This function never rewrites or generates content.

args.**args: Optional keyword arguments providing additional execution context such as workflow step data, expected tone, quality level, domain constraints, or review strictness. These arguments influence evaluation only and never change the content.

review.overall_status: One of: OK, NEEDS IMPROVEMENT, NOT OK.
review.what_is_good: A list describing aspects of the content that meet expectations.
review.what_is_not_good: A list describing gaps, issues, or weaknesses, if any.
review.how_to_improve: Actionable guidance explaining how the content can be improved without rewriting it.

notes: The function only reviews content; it never edits or regenerates it.
Professional tone is always used in the review output.
Medium-quality content is explicitly supported with balanced feedback.
Improvement guidance is descriptive, not prescriptive.
No examples, rewritten content, or answers are included.
Output is execution-safe and suitable for automated workflow decisions.
Designed for use in review, approval, and fallback decision steps.

functions.name: automate.generate_form_schema
functions.description: Generates a structured UI form schema for collecting information from users. The schema describes form fields, their types, validation rules, and optional selectable values so a frontend can dynamically render a data-collection interface. The function produces only schema definitions and never collects or stores user data.

args.user_input: Description of the information that needs to be collected. This acts as the topic or context for generating the form.
args.**args: Optional keyword arguments providing additional context such as workflow step data, domain constraints, required fields, form complexity, or UI preferences.

form.form_id: Unique identifier for the generated form schema.
form.title: Human-readable form title describing the purpose of the form.

fields.label: User-visible label for the field.
fields.type: UI field type. One of: input, textarea, choice, multichoice, boolean, email, phone, date, image.
fields.data_type: Underlying data type. One of: string, number, boolean, url.
fields.required: Boolean indicating whether the field must be filled before submission.
fields.placeholder: Optional hint text displayed in the input field.

notes: This function only generates a form schema; it does not collect, validate, or store user responses.
The output schema is designed to be rendered directly by frontend UI components.
Each field must have a unique 'id'.
Field types must follow the allowed list to ensure compatibility with UI renderers.
Options are included only for 'choice' or 'multichoice' field types.
The schema is execution-safe and suitable for automated workflow-driven UI generation.
Designed for workflows that need structured information gathering from users such as student details, product data, sales forms, surveys, and onboarding flows.

functions.name: automate.assign_or_show_questions_from_file
functions.description: Fetches and returns pre-existing questions from the workflow's stored data (e.g., uploaded questionnaire files). This function acts as a read-only retrieval and view layer, returning the questions as a form-ready questionnaire for display. It does not perform any generation or transformation.

returns.status: Indicates whether the retrieval was successful or failed (string: 'success' or 'error').
returns.questions: List of stored questions formatted for form-based display (array). Includes both normal and MCQ questions exactly as stored. Present only when status is 'success'.
returns.message: Error message explaining why questions could not be retrieved (string). Present only when status is 'error'.

functions.notes: This is a strictly read-only retrieval function used after a questionnaire file has been uploaded and processed earlier in the workflow. It only fetches and returns already stored questions. It must NOT generate new questions, modify existing ones, rephrase content, enrich data, or add/remove fields such as sections or subsections. The output must exactly reflect the stored data and be suitable for direct rendering as a form questionnaire. If no questions are found in the workflow, it must return an error response. For generating new questions, use 'automate.generate_questions'.
